comment.body: Today was my first day  using it, but basically set up the dashboard as shown with gamma heat map on the left, charm on the right, positioning ODTE) in the  middle.  I'd bought a lotto SPX5700 before FOMC at $3, as well as a few SPY566C ODTE, and as price started moving up, you can basically see the probable path with negative gamma above (dealer's buying as spot going up) and supportive charm (same) -- this screenshot is zoomed in a bit towards end of day. So it gave me confidence to hold the lotto a lot longer than I normally would  have, that plus Alma's levels.  So that $3 call for $18, had I been 5 seconds quicker on the sale, it would've been $20.  And several hundred percent on the SPY calls.  I'm not nearly as sophisticated as many on here, and I feel like I am just scratching the surface with the tool, but it's quite useful even for a luddite like me.

comment.body: My thinking (which may not be 100% correct and open to correction) was that once we saw SPX/ESM hold key levels and then the bid start coming in and remain supportive after the 2pm press release, like Alma predicted in her Weekly Post -- and we are in negative gamma [MMs buy with move up in spot] and negative charm [also MM buy with move up in spot], therefore price under the customer and MM buying pressure in this environment is going to be like water flowing towards the lowest point on a surface, here the most negative gamma area (the darker red, and yellow line which marks the bottom of the trough), and that's basically what happened.  It sort of slipped  under and past one suppressive charm area under very negative gamma (the last push to 5715 or so), then gamma became less negative (less MM support) as the bid dried up, price dropped, the next push ran into the suppressive charm (the yellow at 3:50pm or so) and green local gamma high (low absolute value, still negative, but "uphill" to the crest of a little ridge relative to the areas all around it).  Those two together resisted the move higher and price dropped again into supportive charm (blue) and another yellow gamma local low, at the close.
comment.raw_body: My thinking (which may not be 100% correct and open to correction) was that once we saw SPX/ESM hold key levels and then the bid start coming in and remain supportive after the 2pm press release, like Alma predicted in her Weekly Post -- and we are in negative gamma [MMs buy with move up in spot] and negative charm [also MM buy with move up in spot], therefore price under the customer and MM buying pressure in this environment is going to be like water flowing towards the lowest point on a surface, here the most negative gamma area (the darker red, and yellow line which marks the bottom of the trough), and that's basically what happened. Those two together resisted the move higher and price dropped again into supportive charm (blue) and another yellow gamma local low, at the close.
